description: BC kind discrimination (§9.2). Two rules differing only by kind — dirichlet (listed FIRST) and neumann — over a neumann input. A kind-blind matcher (the pre-G8 behavior, where `kind` was dropped on parse) would fire the first rule and emit tag_dirichlet; the correct kind/side matcher skips the dirichlet rule and fires neumann, emitting tag_neumann. Pins that `kind` genuinely participates in matching.
